notes.text: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with chronic kidney disease stage 3. Continue renal monitoring and diabetes management. eGFR from 03/10/2026 reviewed at 42.

Current findings do not support active chronic kidney disease stage 3. Repeat kidney function has improved and prior CKD stage 3 entry appears outdated. Remove active CKD stage 3 from the current assessment and continue routine monitoring.
Type 2 diabetes is active. Patient continues metformin and diet changes. Kidney disease remains on historical problem list; nephrology follow-up is pending.
Renal panel before nephrology visit shows eGFR 42. Results routed to nephrology for interpretation.
Problem list includes chronic kidney disease stage 3 copied from prior nephrology assessment. No independent assessment performed today.
Patient referred for clarification of kidney disease status after prior eGFR in low 40s. Repeat testing and chart review performed.
Diabetes remains active. Reviewed renal specialist update stating CKD stage 3 is not currently supported. Continue diabetes treatment and routine renal monitoring.
Blood pressure 126/74. Patient denies edema, urinary symptoms, chest pain, or shortness of breath.
